sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Wow! Shrinking O by 9 had a huge effect! Not just O changed, but ALL the bars shifted left by 3 cols:
- Y: cols 25-38 → 22-35 (shifted left by 3)
- N: cols 39-41 → 36-38 (shifted left by 3)
- S: cols 24-26 → 21-23 (shifted left by 3)
- O: cols 10-23 → 10-20 (shrank from right, but also the whole L-shape shifted)
- Red dot: (40, 40) → (40, 37) (moved left by 3)
So shrinking O shifts the entire L-shape left by 3 cols! This is a key insight. The O bar controls the horizontal position of the entire L-shape.
Similarly, I should test shrinking S to see if it shifts the entire L-shape up.
Let me also note:
- Y controls the red dot's horizontal position relative to the L-shape (left/right within the L-shape)
- N controls the red dot's vertical position relative to the L-shape (up/down within the L-shape)
- O controls the L-shape's horizontal position (left/right on the board)
- S might control the L-shape's vertical position (up/down on the board)
So to move the red dot from (40, 40) to (31, 52):
- Need to move the L-shape right and up
- Need to adjust the red dot's position within the L-shape
Let me first restore O to its original position, then test shrinking S.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Restore O to original (grow by 9)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 57, 'col': 12}], intent='solve')

seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("After orange right:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['O', 'S', 'Y', 'N'] and n['pixels'] > 7:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  {n['color']}: {n['pixels']}px, rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")

for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'r' and n['pixels'] == 1:
        b = n['boundary']
        print(f"  red dot at ({b[0][0]}, {b[0][1]})")

# Now test shrinking S to see if it shifts the L-shape up
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 57, 'col': 21}], intent='solve')

seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("\nAfter sky blue left:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['O', 'S', 'Y', 'N'] and n['pixels'] > 7:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  {n['color']}: {n['pixels']}px, rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")

for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'r' and n['pixels'] == 1:
        b = n['boundary']
        print(f"  red dot at ({b[0][0]}, {b[0][1]})")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
